response.justification: The candidate is a strong, versatile senior product engineer with genuine testing depth and some high-throughput systems exposure, but he does not meet the core minimum qualifications for this role. The job requires 8 years of C++; his resume shows roughly a year of professional C/C++ (National Instruments, 2014-2015) plus a thesis project, with most of his career in TypeScript/Node.js, DLang, and web product work. His infrastructure experience (low-latency DLang services at Dunnhumby, Kubernetes-deployable open-source platform) is real but falls short of sustained large-scale cloud compute platform ownership, and his leadership evidence is mentoring-flavored (workshops, dojos, courses) rather than leading technical strategy for a platform team.
strengths: Experience with low-latency, high-throughput microservices handling millions of requests per second (Dunnhumby/Sociomantic)
Strong software craftsmanship and testing culture: TDD, CI/CD, authored test frameworks (Fluent-Asserts, Trial), taught testing courses
Broad seniority signal: Staff Engineer at Stripe with recent AI/developer-tooling and full-stack platform contributions
concerns: Falls far short of the 8-year C++ minimum; primary languages are TypeScript/Node.js and DLang, not C++
Limited evidence of owning large-scale cloud compute infrastructure or production cloud architecture over the long term

No formal technical-leadership track record mentoring cloud/DevOps engineers or driving cross-team technical strategy; resume also contains a confusing 'Founder, Datadog' entry that appears to describe an unrelated open-source side project

key_factors.factor: Distributed systems and high-throughput services exposure
Mentoring and testing leadership, but no platform tech-lead role
